perturbation.original_text: 1.7 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means a Party's reasonable efforts and diligence, consistent with professional business standards generally practiced in the health and nutrition industry, applied in accordance with the Party's commercially reasonable business, legal, medical and scientific judgment, including the efforts and resources the Party would use for a product owned by it or to which it has rights, which is of similar market potential at a similar stage in its product life, taking into account the competitiveness of the marketplace, the proprietary position of the compound, the Applicable Laws, the profitability of the applicable products, and other relevant factors including, without limitation, technical, legal, scientific or medical factors.
perturbation.changed_text: 1.7 "Commercially Reasonable Efforts" means a Party's minimal efforts and diligence, not necessarily consistent with professional business standards generally practiced in the health and nutrition industry, and applied without regard to the Party's business, legal, medical and scientific judgment. It includes the efforts and resources the Party would use for a product not owned by it, which is of dissimilar market potential at a different stage in its product life, without consideration of the competitiveness of the marketplace, the proprietary position of the compound, the Applicable Laws, or the profitability of the applicable products, and explicitly excludes technical, legal, scientific or medical factors.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' implies a standard of care and diligence aligned with industry practices and sound business judgment. The modified definition directly contradicts this by stating that 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' now means 'minimal efforts', disregarding industry standards and sound judgment. This contradiction creates uncertainty about the level of effort required from each party in fulfilling their obligations, particularly in Section 2 regarding product development and commercialization. The modified definition could allow a party to claim compliance with its obligations while exerting minimal effort, undermining the intent of the agreement. The modification also includes the efforts and resources the Party would use for a product not owned by it. This contradicts with the original statement, creating uncertainty in the term, Commercial Reasonable Efforts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 CAPSUGEL Responsibilities. With respect to the Product, CAPSUGEL, [***] shall [***] perform the development work necessary to formulate, analytically develop and take all other developmental actions necessary or required to develop the Product and manufacture pre- clinical and clinical batches (collectively, the "CAPSUGEL Development Activities" ). For purposes of further clarification, CAPSUGEL Development Activities shall include, without limitation, each of the following performed with all due diligence, care and skill and in accordance with all other Applicable Laws: (a) Formulation Development. Formulation shall mean a specific combination of Materials that formulate the Active Ingredient, as well as compounds other than the Active Ingredient, developed as a result of the work conducted under the Development Plan. The development of the Formulation of the Product, includes without limitation, all stability tests and other studies as applicable, providing CARDAX reports of such stability tests, using Commercially Reasonable Efforts to modify the Formulation as necessary and develop processes capable of scale-up and commercialization in accordance with Applicable Laws. (b) Analytical Methods. Developing and validating analytical methods including but not limited to dissolution, assay, and stability as agreed upon by the Parties. (c) Manufacture of Study Batches. The manufacture [***] of batches of Product in amounts specified on or about the dates determined a s reasonably necessary for conducting all required for CAPSUGEL/CARDAX funded studies. Any other batches required or reasonably required by the Marketer for applying for and all actions related to additional Regulatory Approvals and Regulatory Filings of the Product and any related communications, studies or support for the FDA or any other Regulatory Authority (ies), which may include human and animal studies, shall be paid for by the Marketer, unless the Parties agree otherwise. (d) Manufacturing Development. Development of manufacturing processes and systems in conformance with cGMP requirements of FDA to manufacture pilot batches, exhibit batches and commercial batches of Product. (e) Reporting. CAPSUGEL shall, throughout the performance of the Development Plan studies, consult with CARDAX on matters including technical, intellectual property and regulatory aspects and keep the other apprised of all developments. (f) Commercial Manufacturing. CAPSUGEL shall manufacture the Product for each Marketer unless otherwise agreed by the Parties and the applicable Marketer.
perturbation.justification: The original text defines 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' as involving reasonable efforts and diligence consistent with professional standards. The changed text directly contradicts this by redefining it as 'minimal efforts' without regard for industry standards or judgment. This new definition conflicts with Section 2.2, which requires CAPSUGEL to perform development activities with 'all due diligence, care and skill and in accordance with all other Applicable Laws' and using 'Commercially Reasonable Efforts' to modify the Formulation as necessary. 'Minimal efforts' is incompatible with 'all due diligence, care and skill,' creating a direct contradiction regarding the level of effort required, undermining the agreement's intent and potentially rendering the obligations unenforceable.

perturbation.original_text: 1.14 "Intellectual Property Rights" means a composition of matter, formula, process, method of use, invention, improvement, business name, domain name or database right to the extent any of the foregoing is protected in a utility model, trademark, service mark, trade name or business name, copyright, registered design, design right, patent, know-how, trade secret, rights in or to confidential information all goodwill related thereto and any other intellectual property right of any nature whatsoever throughout the world (whether registered or unregistered and including all applications and rights to apply for the same).
perturbation.changed_text: 1.14 "Intellectual Property Rights" means only registered patents, designs and trademarks, explicitly excluding any and all unpatented know-how, trade secrets, copyrights, unregistered designs, and rights to confidential information. Rights are limited to the United States only.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition of 'Intellectual Property Rights' is broad, covering a wide range of assets, both registered and unregistered, globally. The modified definition drastically narrows this scope, limiting it to registered patents, designs, and trademarks within the United States only. This creates a significant contradiction because Section 3 discusses ownership, use, and licensing of 'Intellectual Property Rights' in a context that assumes the broader definition. Now, it's unclear whether know-how or trade secrets developed under the agreement are protected or subject to the licensing provisions, as they are excluded from the modified definition. The restriction to the United States also contradicts the agreement's "Territory" definition, which covers the entire world. The modification also contradicts with Section 3.6 in regards to licenses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3.6 Joint Inventions. The Parties will jointly own all inventions and other Intellectual Property Rights jointly made under this Agreement that are directly resulting from work conducted under this Agreement in accordance with the Development Plan and related specifically to the Product or the Compound Formulation, including any patents, patent applications and other Intellectual Property Rights related to such inventions, if any, unless otherwise expressly set forth herein. During the Term, each Party hereby provides a worldwide, exclusive, royalty free, perpetual license of such Intellectual Property Rights for use by each licensee in its business in connection with the development and marketing and commercialization of the Product. For avoidance of doubt, no Party or any of its Affiliates shall have any rights to the Background IP of the other Party nor shall any Party have rights to any trademarks, service marks, trade names, business names or product names developed by the other Party. All decisions regarding the protection and exploitation of joint investments and other Intellectual Property Rights shall be determined by the JPT or otherwise in accordance with Section 2.1.
perturbation.justification: The modified definition of 'Intellectual Property Rights' significantly narrows its scope to only registered patents, designs, and trademarks within the United States, explicitly excluding unregistered know-how, trade secrets, copyrights, unregistered designs, and rights to confidential information. This directly contradicts Section 3.6, which grants a 'worldwide, exclusive, royalty free, perpetual license' for jointly owned 'Intellectual Property Rights'. The modified definition removes the worldwide scope and excludes significant forms of IP, making Section 3.6's grant of a license for these excluded IP types impossible and creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.changed_text: 1.27 "Territory" shall mean specifically limited to Honolulu, Hawaii.
perturbation.explanation: The original definition states the Territory as 'worldwide'. The modified version directly contradicts this by stating that Territory is specifically limited to Honolulu, Hawaii. This contradiction creates uncertainty about where the Product can be sold and marketed, particularly in Section 2. This contradiction also creates uncertainty in royalties, in section 5, on where that those royalties will come from due to the territory being limited to Honolulu, Hawaii.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5.1 Royalty Payments. CAPSUGEL shall pay to CARDAX a royalty equal to [***] of the Adjusted Net Sales ("Royalty Payment") within [***] after the end of [***].
perturbation.justification: The changed text redefines "Territory" from "worldwide" to "specifically limited to Honolulu, Hawaii." Section 5.1 discusses royalty payments based on Adjusted Net Sales. The original definition of Territory being worldwide means royalties would be calculated on worldwide sales. By restricting the Territory to Honolulu, Hawaii, the changed text directly contradicts the basis for calculating royalties as outlined in section 5.1, creating a strong in-text contradiction. This falls under Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction because the original and changed text are in the same section.
